repair:
Rules for Survival of the Literati clans
 1. Literati clans must cooperate with the warlords controlling their homeland. If dissatisfied, they may cause the warlord to "naturally" die...
 2. The purpose of cooperating with warlords is to gain more political resources.
 3. While an individual aristocrat may be weak, the aristocratic clan is vast and inclusive, extending beyond marriage with warlords and dignitaries, turning them into part of their own.
 4. Literati clans collaborate with academic elites to control the commercial resources of a region.
 5. Only by firmly controlling resources can the literati clans gain trust.
 6. If local literati clans cannot merge with incoming ones, they will eliminate them. Externally, literati clans act as a faction, while internally they form cliques. Once external conflicts are resolved, internal divisions arise, splitting the literati clans into multiple forces.

repair:
Tao Qian:
 Tao Qian was appointed by the Eastern Han court as the Inspector of Xuzhou. Historical records state that he led troops from Danyang into Xuzhou when he assumed the position. During this process, Tao Qian did not receive the full support of Xuzhou's local literati clans. To solidify his rule, he relied on Xuzhou's middle factions and particularly trusted the powerful Mi Zhu, hoping to maintain balance between the different factions through his mediation.

Mi Zhu:
 Mi Zhu was a representative of the Great Clans in Xuzhou, rising to prominence due to his merchant background during Tao Qian's reliance on the Danyang faction. To my worthy younger brother Liu Bei:
Our reunion after long separation stirs countless emotions.
In years past, you stood with Tao Qian against my forces, while I was trapped in crisis by Lü Bu's treachery in Yanzhou. At first, I suspected this was your cunning stratagem—using Tao Qian and Lü Bu to weaken me. Yet you have always been upright and honorable; such schemes are not your way. Fortunately, I have resolved the crisis. The past is past, and I hold no grudge.
I still recall our first meeting in Luoyang, where we spoke as heroes of our shared ambitions. That memory remains dear to me. I have never forgotten the kindness you showed in helping me recruit soldiers.
I hear you are busy suppressing the Yellow Turban rebels. Though you have made progress, remnants remain. I am willing to send a detachment of elite infantry to help you wipe them out completely and keep your supply routes secure. Yuan Shao is powerful, and we have an old friendship; I am certain he will receive your goodwill. May fortune favor you. If you have need, speak at any time.
In any case, may you fare well. I look forward to joining hands once more to build a great enterprise together.
Cao Cao
With sincere respect

repair:
Council is the core process where the Governor of Xuzhou convenes subordinate officials and representatives from all sides to deliberate on affairs. Whether a bill can be smoothly enacted depends on the support of the major factions under your command.
During the council, participants are divided into three categories: supporters, opponents, and undecided. The undecided factions, whose stance is not yet fixed and can be swayed, are the key to tipping the vote.
As the Governor of a province, you hold the highest authority and nominally have the power to make decisions on your own. However, for decrees to be implemented smoothly without internal obstruction, you cannot rely solely on personal orders. Before officially issuing a decree, you must repeatedly consult internally to secure the approval of the majority of factions. Without faction support, the forces under your command will passively resist, making it difficult to enforce the decree.
You can privately meet with each faction in your residence to negotiate and lobby, increasing the likelihood of a bill's passage. Through mediation, factions that were originally staunchly opposed to the bill will weigh the pros and cons, shift to a neutral stance, and become swayable undecided forces.
